chunks.text: Eugene Wong is Professor Emeritus in the EECS Department at UC Berkeley. He received his B.S., A.M., and Ph.D. degrees in Electrical Engineering from Princeton University in 1955, 1958, and 1959, respectively. Prior to joining Berkeley, he was an NSF Postdoctoral Fellow at University of Cambridge (1959-1960) and a researcher at IBM Research Center in Yorktown, New York (1960-1962). He joined the EECS faculty in 1962, researching database management systems, optimization algorithms, stochastic processes, and neural networks. From 1985-1989, he served as Department Chair, leading significant growth. He retired in 1994 as Professor Emeritus.
chunks.question: What are Eugene Wong's educational background and early career positions?
chunks.answer: Princeton degrees, Cambridge fellow, IBM researcher.

chunks.text: Prof. Wong's career includes academic, business, and public service contributions. Academically, he authored over 100 articles, published four books, and supervised over 20 dissertations. In business, he co-founded Relational Technology, Inc. (later INGRES Corporation) in 1980, was CEO of SuperNet, Ltd. and founder of Asia Internet Holding Company in Hong Kong (1994-1996), and held roles at Versata, Inc. (1998-2005). His public service includes roles in the U.S. Office of Science and Technology Policy (1990-1993), Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (1994-1996), NSF (1998-2000), and various advisory committees.
chunks.question: What are Eugene Wong's key achievements in academia, business, and public service?
chunks.answer: Authored articles, co-founded INGRES, held government roles.

chunks.text: Prof. Wong is a member and former Councilor for the National Academy of Engineering, a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Science, a Lifetime Member of the IEEE, a Member of the ACM, and an Academician of the Academia Sinica (Taiwan). He received the ACM Software System Award in 1988 for his work on INGRES and the 2005 IEEE Founders Medal for leadership in engineering research and technology policy. His research area is Information, Data, Network, and Communication Sciences (IDNCS).
chunks.question: What honors and memberships does Eugene Wong hold?
chunks.answer: NAE member, IEEE Founders Medal, ACM award.

chunks.text: Selected publications by Eugene Wong include: 'An economic case for basic research' in Nature (1996), 'Decomposition--A strategy for query processing' in ACM Trans. Database Systems (1976), 'The design and implementation of INGRES' in ACM Trans. Database Systems (1976), 'Martingales and stochastic integrals for processes with a multidimensional parameter' in Zeitschrift fur Wahrscheinlichkeitstheorie (1974), 'Some results concerning the zero-crossings of Gaussian noise' in SIAM J. Applied Mathematics (1966), 'On the convergence of ordinary integrals to stochastic integrals' in The Annals of Mathematical Statistics (1965), and 'A linear search problem' in SIAM Review (1964).
chunks.question: What are some of Eugene Wong's notable publications?
chunks.answer: INGRES design, query processing, stochastic integrals papers.
